meaning: adv.含糊不清地,引起歧异地
examples: The last paragraph is ambiguously phrased.
The sentence is so ambiguously worded that it admits of two interpretations.
As the camera slowly pans in, Jesse smiles and ambiguously responds, "I know."
Right now we are somewhere between the first two scenarios -- and could remain ambiguously so for some time.
Ever had a dispute with your life insurer over an ambiguously worded policy?
